SHOPENHANCEMENT-REMOVE_BASE_COST.title: Base Cost
SHOPENHANCEMENT-REMOVE_BASE_COST.hover.d: The original price is 75 + 25*n. If you want the full original experience, you can increase it to 75.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-REMOVE_STEP_COST.title: Step Cost
SHOPENHANCEMENT-REFRESH_COST.title: Refresh Cost
SHOPENHANCEMENT-RELIC_REFRESH_MODE.title: Shop Relic Refresh Logic
SHOPENHANCEMENT-RELIC_REFRESH_MODE.hover:
Vanilla: Relics do not repeat once shown, even if they were not purchased. After enough refreshes, only Circlets may remain (around 40 refreshes).
Rotation Queue: Prioritizes unseen relics. Once all candidates have been seen, unacquired relics return to the refresh pool and may appear again.
Fully Random: Randomly selects from all eligible, unpurchased relic candidates.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ENABLE_SELL_MODE.title: Enable Sell Mode
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ENABLE_SELL_MODE.hover.d: Click the Sell Mode button in the bottom-left corner of the shop screen, then right-click a relic or potion to sell it. Note: the prices below are not the shop's buying prices, but the gold you earn when selling to the shop.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-SELL_PRICE_VARIANCE.hove: Relic and potion sell prices are randomly offset by up to ± the set value, calculated independently per item. 0.20 means at most ±20%.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-RELIC_SELL_RULE_PRESET.h:
Selecting a preset updates all rule toggles below. Manually changing any rule returns this setting to Custom. The checks use the relic markers supplied by the game. Not every relic has been verified, so actual results may occasionally differ from expectations; refer to the changes shown in the rules below.
All rules are evaluated in parallel. A relic is blocked if any matching property is configured as not sellable, and every failed property is listed in the popup. Starter, Ancient, and Event are mutually exclusive properties of each relic, not mutually exclusive settings; you may enable or disable all three settings together.
Strict Mode (not recommended) follows the vanilla relic tradability marker. It blocks a relic if its uses are exhausted, its effect triggers on pickup, it is melted, it spawns pets, or its rarity is Starter, Ancient, or Event. Actual behavior is determined by the game's relic tradability marker; this list is only an equivalent reference.
When a Toy Box is sold, the mod preserves its remaining combat counter and continues its original wax-relic melting lifecycle after sale.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ALLOW_STARTER_RELICS.hov: Example: Burning Blood. Starter, Ancient, and Event are mutually exclusive properties of a relic, not mutually exclusive settings; all three toggles may be on or off together.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ALLOW_ANCIENT_RELICS.hov: Ancient relics are the Ancients' relics found throughout the acts, such as Golden Compass.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ALLOW_EVENT_RELICS.title: Allow Event Relics
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ALLOW_EVENT_RELICS.hover: Examples: Big Mushroom and Blessed Cheese.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ALLOW_WAX_RELICS.title: Allow Wax Relics
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ALLOW_WAX_RELICS.hover.d: Checks only the game's IsWax marker. This setting is evaluated alongside the melted-relic setting. The valid Wax/Melted combinations are On/On, On/Off, and Off/Off.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ALLOW_MELTED_RELICS.hove: This setting is evaluated alongside the wax-relic setting. Enabling it also enables Allow Wax Relics; disabling Allow Wax Relics also disables this setting.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ALLOW_USED_UP_RELICS.hov: Example: Lizard Tail restores 50% health on death. After triggering, the game marks it as used up and this rule can prevent its sale.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ALLOW_UPON_PICKUP_RELICS: Example: Strawberry increases maximum health when obtained. Disable this to prevent selling it after its upon-pickup effect has already resolved.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-ALLOW_DISABLED_RELICS.ho: Uses the game's Disabled relic status. Example: Pumpkin Candle is disabled after five combats and becomes enabled again when rekindled at a rest site.
For reference: the in-game common relic price is 175; the default is roughly 40% of that.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-SELL_UNCOMMON_RELIC_PRIC: For reference: the in-game uncommon relic price is 225; the default is roughly 40% of that.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-SELL_RARE_RELIC_PRICE.ho: For reference: the in-game rare relic price is 275; the default is roughly 40% of that.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-SELL_SHOP_RELIC_PRICE.ho: For reference: the in-game shop-pool relic price is 200; the default is roughly 40% of that.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-SELL_ANCIENT_RELIC_PRICE: The game does not assign a price to ancient relics. Considering rarity and balance, the default is based on 600 at roughly 40%.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-SELL_STARTER_RELIC_PRICE: The game does not assign a price to starter relics. Considering rarity and balance, the default is based on 600 at roughly 40%.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-SELL_EVENT_RELIC_PRICE.h: The game does not assign a price to event relics. Considering rarity and balance, the default is based on 200 at roughly 40%.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-SELL_COMMON_POTION_PRICE: For reference: the in-game common potion price is 50; the default is roughly 40% of that.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-SELL_UNCOMMON_POTION_PRI: For reference: the in-game uncommon potion price is 75; the default is roughly 40% of that.
SHOPENHANCEMENT-SELL_RARE_POTION_PRICE.h: For reference: the in-game rare potion price is 100; the default is roughly 40% of that.
